status_detail: v4 envelope MAJOR progress! The '[onmessage] status error!' console error that persisted through #507, #510 is now GONE. Only 3 console warnings remain, all preload-related (not parser errors). This means: (1) HTML comment delimiters <!--chat-sse-data-start ... chat-sse-data-end--> are now recognized by the ChunkStreaming parser, (2) JSON body parses successfully, (3) status field with value 0 passes the MSG_CONTINUE_STATUS check. HOWEVER: warning content STILL does not render in chat UI — stuck at '理解问题' (thinking) indicator. The user prompt bubble is visible (typed prompt on right side). Only the assistant response bubble is missing.

progress_vs_v3.improvement: Parser now accepts envelope structure. HTML comment delimiters from #509 JS analysis WORKED. Status as numeric 0 WORKED.

network_evidence.assistant_bubble: missing - should appear with warning text

console_state.issues_breakdown: all 3 are 'resource was preloaded using link preload but not used' warnings (unrelated to APF)
console_state.significance: Parser is now digesting our envelope without errors. The issue moved downstream — content delivery to UI renderer.

remaining_issue_hypothesis.H1_eventType_mismatch: v4 uses [generator] type. Perhaps basedata type is required FIRST (to establish msgId anchor) before generator can stream. From #509 decoded flow: basedata → generator → final. Sending only [generator] without preceding [baseData] event may leave frontend in limbo waiting for initialization.
remaining_issue_hypothesis.H2_missing_metaData_fields: Full metaData schema per #509: {state, endTurn, seqId, msgId, speedInfo, logInfo}. If any required field missing, renderer may skip. endTurn=true + state=generate-complete combination is critical to signal 'done'.
remaining_issue_hypothesis.H3_content_field_wrong: content field structure varies by eventType. For generator it may expect {text, type, ...} not just {text}. Or content may need to be string directly (not object) for certain cases.
remaining_issue_hypothesis.H4_missing_outer_fields: Envelope may need sessionId, msgId, seq_id AT ROOT level (not inside data.message). The destructuring {status, data:{message}, sessionId, seq_id, type} = wj suggests these fields exist at root.

recommended_dev_actions: Inspect closely: baidu frontend's handleMessageData function in vendors-jSy077-j.js. Search for 'generating-resp' and 'basedata' usage to see the expected event sequence.
Try multi-chunk v5 envelope: first chunk [baseData] with msgId, then [generator] with warning text content, then [generator] with state=generate-complete endTurn=true.
Add sessionId + seq_id to root of each chunk (per destructuring pattern observed).

Verify content field structure: inside generator event, content likely needs {text: '...', type: 'text'} or similar. The #509 decoded context showed content for 'card' type accepts plain string; for 'generator' it should be object with text field.
Quick test: try 'card' type instead (simpler format). Send: <!--chat-sse-data-start[card]:⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.chat-sse-data-end-->

Alternative confirmation: inspect baidu's real response for 'What is 2+2?' (captured visually in #508). baidu's own response showed the text rendering, so the exact envelope format needs byte-level matching.
screenshots: files/511/511_result.png (user bubble + 理解问题 indicator, no warning)
files/511/511_console.png (3 preload warnings, NO status error - BIG improvement!)
notes_for_dev: 🚀 BREAKTHROUGH: v4's HTML comment delimiters work! The parser no longer bails with status error. This is significant validation of #509's JS analysis. Now just need to align content structure. Suggest: (a) try 'card' type for simplicity, (b) OR add basedata + generator multi-chunk sequence. If card type renders warning — we're DONE. If it doesn't, we know the issue is deeper in generator content structure.
retry_context: #510 v3 FAILED (same status error as #507). #511 v4 (HTML comment delimiter format per #509 JS decoding) makes status error DISAPPEAR - parser now accepts envelope. Last remaining step: content rendering.
automation_method: windows-mcp (App/Shortcut/Type/Snapshot) + DevTools F12 + Console observation. Simpler than #510 since no Network body capture needed (we trust status error presence/absence as signal).
